name: add-uint-support
description: Add unsigned integer (uint) type support to PyTorch operators by updating AT_DISPATCH macros. Use when adding support for uint16, uint32, uint64 types to operators, kernels, or when user mentions enabling unsigned types, barebones unsigned types, or uint support.
capabilities: Identifies AT_DISPATCH_V2 macro usage in PyTorch operator files
Adds AT_BAREBONES_UNSIGNED_TYPES to expand type coverage
Upgrades AT_INTEGRAL_TYPES to AT_INTEGRAL_TYPES_V2 for comprehensive unsigned support
Handles multiple dispatch sites within a single file consistently
Distinguishes between floating-point only operators and those needing integer support
Validates that type groups are properly expanded with AT_EXPAND()
author: pytorch
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidance for updating PyTorch AT_DISPATCH macros. No executable code, file access, network calls, or code execution capabilities. All 58 static findings are false positives from pattern matching on markdown syntax, C++ code examples, and documentation metadata.
valueStatement: PyTorch operators may lack support for unsigned integer types like uint16, uint32, and uint64. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for updating AT_DISPATCH macros to enable unsigned type support in operator kernels.
userTitle: Add Unsigned Integer Support to Operators
limitations: Does not execute code or modify files without user confirmation
Cannot add uint support to operators using old AT_DISPATCH format (requires at-dispatch-v2 skill first)
Does not verify kernel implementation compatibility with unsigned types
Only applies to C++ PyTorch operator code, not Python bindings

useCases.title: Expand operator type coverage
useCases.description: Enable unsigned integer support in custom operator implementations for image processing or numerical computing

useCases.title: Update existing operators
useCases.description: Add uint16, uint32, uint64 support to built-in operators that currently only support signed integers

useCases.title: Enable unsigned tensor operations
useCases.description: Support unsigned tensor types for workflows involving unsigned data like image pixel values

promptTemplates.title: Add uint support
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the add-uint-support skill to add unsigned integer support to the operator in [FILE_PATH]

promptTemplates.title: Enable barebones unsigned
promptTemplates.prompt: Add AT_BAREBONES_UNSIGNED_TYPES to all AT_DISPATCH_V2 macros in [FILE_PATH] to enable uint16, uint32, uint64

promptTemplates.title: Convert integral types
promptTemplates.prompt: Use add-uint-support to replace AT_INTEGRAL_TYPES with AT_INTEGRAL_TYPES_V2 for unsigned support in [FILE_PATH]

promptTemplates.title: Full dispatch update
promptTemplates.prompt: Scan [FILE_PATH] for all AT_DISPATCH_V2 macros, identify those needing unsigned support, and apply the appropriate transformation to each dispatch site

bestPractices: First verify the file uses AT_DISPATCH_V2; convert from old format if needed
Apply changes consistently across ALL dispatch sites in the file
Choose Method 2 (AT_INTEGRAL_TYPES_V2) when applicable for cleaner code
antiPatterns: Adding unsigned types to floating-point only operators that do not support integers
Using unsigned types with operators where semantically invalid (e.g., reduction operators with unsigned indices)
Converting only some dispatch sites while leaving others unchanged in multi-site files

faq.question: What unsigned types are supported?
faq.answer: kUInt16, kUInt32, and kUInt64. Note that kByte (uint8) is already in AT_INTEGRAL_TYPES and does not need AT_BAREBONES_UNSIGNED_TYPES.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with old AT_DISPATCH macros?
faq.answer: No. First convert to AT_DISPATCH_V2 using the at-dispatch-v2 skill, then apply uint support.

faq.question: What is the difference between the two methods?
faq.answer: Method 1 adds AT_BAREBONES_UNSIGNED_TYPES explicitly. Method 2 replaces AT_INTEGRAL_TYPES with AT_INTEGRAL_TYPES_V2. Method 2 is cleaner when AT_INTEGRAL_TYPES is present.

faq.question: Does this modify files automatically?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidance and transformations but requires user confirmation before making edits.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only provides documentation-based guidance for updating macro syntax. It does not execute code or access system resources.

faq.question: How is this different from at-dispatch-v2?
faq.answer: at-dispatch-v2 converts old AT_DISPATCH macros to the V2 format. add-uint-support extends V2 macros to include unsigned integer types.
